free_text: The optical model for elastic scattering of 10 to 15
MeV polarized deuterons from medium-weight nuclei
telescope consisted of a dE detector 104-124 mu-m thick
Two monitor detectors, mounted at 13.1 degr. to the
left and right of the beam, provided the run-length
The total number of deuterons scattered into these
detectors during a run is proportional to the number of
incident deuterons, to the target thickness, and to the
elastic cross section at 13.1 degr. The cross sections
were calculated from the ratios of the number of
deuterons detected by the telescopes to the number
detected by the monitor detectors, making use of the
known solid angle ratios and the assumed cross sections
at 13.1 degr., which were taken from optical
* for the dead time of the analog-to-digital
* for the effects of unresolved contaminants in
* for the multiple small-angle scattering from the
target into the monitor detectors, and
* for the finite size of the detectors.
uncertainties, but not including uncertainties in the
overall normalization of each angular distribution
Data from the tables of R.P.Goddard's thesis
